LOCK IN — Warehouse Loading Training That Plays Like a Game
Source: https://trailer-load.com/  ·  Made by F-Keys (founder: Vince Gonzalez)

=== WHAT IT IS ===
LOCK IN is a free, browser-based warehouse trailer-loading training simulator. Pieces ride in on a belt; you stack them into a trailer wall that has to physically hold up. It plays like a puzzle game but models real freight-loading: support and balance, load integrity, locking complete rows into shelves, hazmat placed low, fragile on top, non-conveyables staged, dead air stuffed inside tires, and a dispatch clock counting down. Every wall is scored on integrity, density, pace, and safety, and ranked from Rookie to Yard Legend.

It runs in any browser with no install and no login required. Gameplay is always free. Players can optionally create a free account to save their record, and can separately opt in to share their stats with a facility.

=== HOW IT WORKS ===
1. Build the wall — Select freight, place it, rotate it. Floor-up, support underneath, no floating boxes. The physics won't let you cheat gravity.
2. Load it right — Lock shelves, keep the center of gravity centered, fragile on top, hazmat below the waist line, and stuff dead air inside tires.
3. Get your grade — Every wall is scored on integrity, density, pace, and safety, then ranked from Rookie to Yard Legend.

Modes: Practice (learn at your own pace), Arcade (full-tempo scored shift), Tour (graduating level scenarios at real hubs), Daily (one shared deterministic board for everyone, 00:00–23:59 UTC), Gauntlet (pure stacking, no mercy).

Companion mini-game: Smalls Sort (https://trailer-load.com/ss/) — sorting and stacking small parcels, testing speed and accuracy on the smalls line.

=== BUSINESS MODEL: FREE FOR WORKERS, DASHBOARD FOR FACILITIES ===
The game is free forever. What schools and employers pay for is the data.

Workers play free:
- Full game, every mechanic, no paywall.
- Optional free account to keep your record.
- You decide if your stats are saved.
- You decide, separately, if a facility can see them.

Facilities get the metrics (the paid product):
- A dashboard of opted-in players' loading performance.
- Load integrity (attention to detail), completed walls (follow-through), throughput (pieces per hour), and safety incidents — the numbers managers already track — per student, sortable, with CSV export.
- Built for CTE programs (Perkins-eligible), trade and technical schools, and warehouse operations teams.
- Provisioned by F-Keys when a program comes aboard.

Who it's for: CTE high-school programs, trade and technical schools, warehouse and dock operations teams, logistics employers, workforce-readiness programs.

=== IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER ===
Training supplement only. LOCK IN builds intuition and tracks readiness. It does NOT replace employer safety training, OSHA training, or any certification requirement.

=== ABOUT ===
LOCK IN is made by F-Keys (https://www.f-keys.com/), a creative technology studio. Founder: Vince Gonzalez. Contact: hello@trailer-load.com.
